steps.title: Follow the distancing logic
steps.content: Richter believed theater should produce an intellectual rather than emotional response. Carlisle's play follows that influence by distancing audiences while asking them to interrogate patriarchy and colonialism.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported outcomes
steps.content:
• Choice A: Focuses on characters' beliefs, but the passage emphasizes issues beyond onstage characters.
  • Choice B: Says the reflection is unrelated to patriarchy and colonialism, contradicting the text.
  • Choice C: Invents a Germany-England historical comparison.
  • Choice D: Connects emotional distance with critical thought about the play's political questions.
The answer is D.
